programs.name: CampTO Plus: Creative Writing
programs.description: This camp is for those who have a story to tell. Come share your stories with us as you learn the basics of writing with a focus on thought organization, plot and character development.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your creative writing skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Advanced (On the Road)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is great way to take your cycling to the next level. Learn how to ride safely and skillfully on residential streets, through guided practice and instruction. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cycling skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Beginner
programs.description: For brand-new and beginner riders this camp is great way to learn to bicycle. Through skill introduction, drills and games, you will practice how to balance, pedal, steer and stop all while remaining on-site.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cyclying  skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet, and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Beginner
programs.description: For brand-new and beginner riders this camp is great way to learn to bicycle. Through skill introduction, drills and games, you will practice how to balance, pedal, steer and stop all while remaining on-site.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cyclying  skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet, and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Intermediate (Bike Handling Skills)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is a great way to take your cycling to the next level. Through skills, drills and games, you will learn how to do hand signals, shoulder checks, steer, turn and change gears while on-site or in a non-vehicular park.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your bike cycling skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet, and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Tiny Tots Swim
programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
